title: HUF 7-Axis Systemic Flaw Detection — OWID World Energy Data
design_principle: HUF never fails. The system HUF analyzes has flaws. HUF finds them. Every direction produces a detection, not a grade.

framing_correction.wrong_framing: SF-001/SF-002 graded HUF as pass/fail. '3 out of 7 directions pass → INSUFFICIENT.' This treats HUF as the subject being tested.
framing_correction.right_framing: HUF is the examiner. Every direction probes a different structural property. Every result is a detection. The 7-direction battery is a flaw-finding protocol, not a certification exam.
framing_correction.corrected_by: Peter Higgins: 'HUF never fails, the system HUF analyzes has flaws we find systemic flaws.'

D1_observable_compression.question: Can the observables be compressed?
D1_observable_compression.detection: 12 energy share columns contain only 4 independent dimensions. 8 of 12 observables are redundant — they are linear combinations of 4 underlying root structures.
D1_observable_compression.flaw_found: OBSERVABLE REDUNDANCY — the OWID dataset carries 3x more columns than it has information. Any analysis treating all 12 share columns as independent is overcounting degrees of freedom.
D1_observable_compression.what_huf_protects_against: Researchers who run 12 separate regressions on 12 share columns are finding the same 4 things twelve times. HUF detects this before analysis begins.

D2_element_identity.question: Are carrier elements distinguishable by root fingerprint?
D2_element_identity.result: 58.2% identified at rank 1, 84.8% at rank 5. Mean rank = 3.4
D2_element_identity.detection: Most countries have unique energy fingerprints in 4-root space, but 15 countries (19%) are structurally indistinguishable from their nearest neighbor at this resolution.
D2_element_identity.flaw_found: PARTIAL IDENTITY COLLAPSE — countries like Indonesia, Chile, Brazil, and Ireland have energy mixes that are not unique in root space. They are structurally interchangeable at 4-root resolution.
D2_element_identity.what_huf_protects_against: Policy analysis that treats every country as structurally distinct when some countries are actually energy-mix clones of each other. If Indonesia and another country have the same root fingerprint, they face the same structural risks.

D3_carrier_idiosyncrasy.question: Can carrier elements substitute for each other?
D3_carrier_idiosyncrasy.detection: Countries are genuinely idiosyncratic. No small set of 'representative' countries can stand in for the full 79. But the ROW SPACE itself compresses: 4 row-roots capture 76% of the country-to-country variation.
D3_carrier_idiosyncrasy.flaw_found: CARRIER ELEMENT UNIQUENESS — each country's energy mix is structurally unique. K_eff=11 means 11 effective dimensions in the carrier space, but those dimensions cannot be instantiated by 11 literal countries. The carrier simplex is not a set of exemplars.
D3_carrier_idiosyncrasy.what_huf_protects_against: The temptation to use 'representative countries' (e.g., 'China represents all developing economies'). No country represents any other country's energy structure. Aggregate analyses that substitute regional representatives for actual countries are structurally invalid.
D3_carrier_idiosyncrasy.key_insight: K_eff counts effective DIMENSIONS, not effective ELEMENTS. This distinction must be formalized in HUF ontology.

D4_domain_boundary.question: Do observable domains see the same underlying structure?
D4_domain_boundary.result: share→percap R²=0.49, percap→share R²=0.49. share→abs R²=0.08. abs→share R²=0.02.
D4_domain_boundary.detection: TWO distinct structural domains exist in this dataset. Domain A (composition space): shares and per-capita measures are mutually predictive at R²≈0.49 — they see the same energy-mix structure. Domain B (scale space): absolute TWh measures see a completely different structure (country SIZE, not country MIX).
D4_domain_boundary.flaw_found: DOMAIN BOUNDARY at the simplex wall. Composition observables and scale observables are structurally incoherent — they live in different spaces. Mixing them in the same analysis conflates 'what kind of energy' with 'how much energy.' These are orthogonal questions.
D4_domain_boundary.what_huf_protects_against: Any analysis that mixes share columns with absolute columns (e.g., regressing coal_share on coal_consumption) is crossing a domain boundary. The variables will correlate, but the correlation is driven by SCALE, not STRUCTURE. HUF's carrier discipline (normalize to simplex first) eliminates this confound.
D4_domain_boundary.key_insight: The cross-prediction ceiling (R²≈0.49) tells us exactly how much of composition is predictable from per-capita and vice versa. The 51% that isn't shared is the gap between 'what fraction is coal' and 'how much coal per person' — these overlap but are not the same.

D5_temporal_structure.question: How many eras does the global energy system need?
D5_temporal_structure.result: 3 eras capture 96.5% of 24 years of temporal variation
D5_temporal_structure.detection: The global energy system from 2000-2023 has THREE structural eras. Era 1: pre-2008 (fossil expansion, coal rising). Era 2: 2008-2019 (gas transition, early renewables). Era 3: 2020-2023 (COVID disruption + accelerated renewable deployment). The transitions align with known events: 2008 financial crisis, 2020 pandemic.
D5_temporal_structure.flaw_found: TEMPORAL REGIME STRUCTURE — the system is NOT a smooth trend. It reorganizes at discrete moments. Any time-series model that assumes smooth continuous change misses the regime boundaries.
D5_temporal_structure.what_huf_protects_against: Linear trend analysis. If someone fits a straight line to 2000-2023 energy data, they're averaging across 3 structurally different regimes. The 2008 transition and the 2020 transition are not noise — they're structural reorganizations.

era_transitions.interpretation: Post-COVID rebound + gas crisis → largest structural jump
Ukraine energy crisis restructures European mix
COVID demand collapse, temporary oil/coal drop

D6_noise_floor.question: How much of the system is structurally predictable vs irreducibly noisy?
D6_noise_floor.result: Recovery R² = 0.09 at 10% missing, 0.30 at 20%, 0.15 at 30%
D6_noise_floor.detection: 77% of variance is captured by 4 roots (forward direction), but individual cell values are NOT recoverable from structure alone. The 23% residual is NOT random noise — it is IDIOSYNCRATIC structure at the country×observable level that cannot be compressed further.
D6_noise_floor.flaw_found: IRREDUCIBLE IDIOSYNCRASY — 23% of each country's energy mix is genuinely unique to that country and cannot be predicted from the global root structure. This is the noise floor of the system.
D6_noise_floor.what_huf_protects_against: Overconfidence in imputation. If someone fills in missing energy data using a structural model, they'll get the broad strokes right (77%) but the country-specific details wrong (23%). HUF quantifies exactly how much trust to place in imputed values.
D6_noise_floor.key_insight: The noise floor (23%) is itself a finding. It tells us that roughly 1/4 of each country's energy structure is unexplainable by global patterns — it's the fingerprint of local geology, policy, history, and accident. That fraction is remarkably stable across the missing-data percentages, suggesting it's a genuine system property.

D7_eigenvalue_degeneracy.question: Which roots are intrinsic to the system and which are sample artifacts?
D7_eigenvalue_degeneracy.result: PC1 stability = 0.87 (rock solid). PC2 = 0.41, PC3 = 0.42, PC4 = 0.22 (unstable)
D7_eigenvalue_degeneracy.detection: The DOMINANT root (fossil vs clean energy, 41% of variance) is a robust, intrinsic property of the global energy system — it survives any random subsetting. The THREE MINOR roots are a rotationally degenerate 3D subspace — they describe 36% of variance collectively, but their individual axes can rotate freely without changing the total.
D7_eigenvalue_degeneracy.flaw_found: EIGENVALUE DEGENERACY in roots 2-4. Eigenvalues 1.77, 1.40, 1.20 are too close to resolve into unique axes at n=79. The system truly has 1 strong root + a 3D 'everything else' subspace, not 4 distinct interpretable roots.
D7_eigenvalue_degeneracy.what_huf_protects_against: Over-interpretation of minor components. If someone names PC2 'solar vs gas' and builds policy around it, they're interpreting a rotational artifact. The SUBSPACE is real (36% of variance exists in that 3D block) but the individual AXES within it are not. HUF's stability test catches this before anyone builds theory on sand.
D7_eigenvalue_degeneracy.key_insight: Root count (4) is stable. Root 1 meaning ('fossil vs clean') is stable. Roots 2-4 are a stable SUBSPACE with unstable internal axes. HUF should report this as: '1 resolved root + 1 unresolved 3D subspace = 4 degrees of freedom total.' The distinction between resolved and unresolved roots is a new HUF concept this test discovered.

resolved_vs_unresolved_roots.definition: A RESOLVED root has stable eigenvector identity across subsamples (cosine > 0.7). An UNRESOLVED root belongs to a degenerate subspace where axes can rotate. Root COUNT includes both; root INTERPRETATION applies only to resolved roots.
resolved_vs_unresolved_roots.implication: HUF reports should distinguish '1 resolved root + 1 unresolved 3D subspace' from '4 interpretable roots.' Both describe 4 degrees of freedom, but the interpretive confidence is different.

domain_boundary_wall.definition: A DOMAIN BOUNDARY exists where cross-prediction R² drops below ~0.1. It marks the point where one observable set ceases to see the same structure as another. For OWID: the boundary is between composition space (shares, per-capita) and scale space (absolute TWh).
domain_boundary_wall.implication: HUF's carrier discipline (normalize to simplex) is not a convention — it's a structural necessity. The simplex wall IS the domain boundary.

noise_floor_measurement.definition: The NOISE FLOOR is the fraction of variance that cannot be recovered from root structure, even with complete forward reconstruction data. For OWID: 23%. This represents irreducible country-level idiosyncrasy.
noise_floor_measurement.implication: The noise floor is a system property, not a model limitation. It tells you the maximum achievable prediction accuracy for any model on this data.

carrier_dimension_vs_carrier_element.definition: K_eff counts effective DIMENSIONS of the carrier space, not effective ELEMENTS. 11 dimensions ≠ 11 representative countries. The carrier simplex compresses dimensionally but not elementwise.
carrier_dimension_vs_carrier_element.implication: HUF must distinguish 'how many independent directions exist in the carrier space' from 'how many carrier elements are needed.' They are different questions with different answers.

owid_system_health_report.title: OWID World Energy Data — Systemic Health Report via HUF 7-Axis Detection
summary: 1. OBSERVABLE REDUNDANCY: 12 share columns contain only 4 independent dimensions (3:1 redundancy).
2. PARTIAL IDENTITY COLLAPSE: 19% of countries are structurally indistinguishable at 4-root resolution.
3. CARRIER IDIOSYNCRASY: Countries cannot substitute for each other. K_eff counts dimensions, not exemplars.
4. DOMAIN BOUNDARY: Composition space and scale space are structurally incoherent. Mixing them conflates mix with magnitude.
5. TEMPORAL REGIME STRUCTURE: 3 discrete eras (pre-2008, 2008-2019, 2020-2023), not a smooth trend.
6. IRREDUCIBLE NOISE FLOOR: 23% of variance is country-specific idiosyncrasy, unpredictable from global structure.
7. EIGENVALUE DEGENERACY: 1 resolved root (fossil vs clean) + 1 unresolved 3D subspace. Minor roots are rotationally ambiguous.
what_any_analyst_should_know_before_touc: Your 12 share columns are really 4 things measured 12 ways.
Don't mix shares with absolute TWh in the same model.
Don't fit linear trends across 2008 or 2020 — the system reorganized there.
Don't assume country X 'represents' a group — every country is unique.
Your achievable prediction ceiling is ~77%, not 100%. The other 23% is real idiosyncrasy.
If you interpret PC2-4 individually, you're reading tea leaves. Only PC1 (fossil vs clean) is stable.
